column5: Brief Rationale (multi-angle: operational + economic + governance + permissionlessness)
Gold standard PoW; global miner distribution; no team/foundation; 16+ years battle-tested; highest permissionlessness.
Top validator/node count + client diversity; PoS stake risks (Lido concentration) but vast factions and on-chain governance.
Binance ecosystem influence; limited/controlled validators; low Nakamoto.
Ripple Labs/foundation heavy control; permissioned validator list.
Large validator set but PoH hardware barriers; Nakamoto ~12; improving but trade-offs.
Justin Sun dominance; low validator diversity.
Tokenized HELOC RWA by Figure (issuer-controlled on Provenance).
PoW BTC fork; similar high miner distribution and permissionlessness.
High validators + delegation; on-chain governance; Nakamoto ~22.
Hyperliquid L1 (perp DEX); relatively new validator distribution.
Privacy-first PoW; ASIC-resistant; excellent censorship resistance.
Decentralized oracle nodes; staking but data-source dependencies.
Canton Network (institutional TradFi L1); consortium governance.
Memecore (new L1 for memes); early-stage L1 risks.
Established PoW; permissionless like BTC but smaller scale.
PoW with privacy; solid but smaller miner base.
Decentralized prediction markets on Arbitrum; DAO + permissionless.
Memecoin; whale concentration + no formal governance.
Modern Move L1; solid but newer validator risks.
Bittensor (AI); subnet validators but concentration risks.
Crypto.com exchange ties; limited independent control.
Gold-backed; floats with metal but Tether issuer elements.

Highest PoS Nakamoto (~123); nominated validators + parachains.

Sky Protocol (MakerDAO successor); strong DeFi DAO on ETH.
Aster L2; newer with sequencer/governance risks.
Sharded L1; Nakamoto ~9; improving but trade-offs.

Pump.fun (meme launchpad); community but platform risks.

Obscure low-cap token; typical early concentration.
Chiliz sports fan tokens; platform centralization.
